riskFloorReasons: safety-invariant proximity: src/messaging/TelegramAdapter.ts matches Telegram adapter
new capability: new config key added

causalAutopsy.notes: The leak path ('this.config.dashboardPin || "(check your config)"') was original code in broadcastDashboardUrl — never exercised until the dashboard PIN failed to resolve at boot. v1.3.270 added a {secret:true}-object normalization for config.token after the boot-crash incident (the same unresolved-secret failure mode) but did NOT cover config.dashboardPin, leaving the PIN broadcast exposed. The trigger on 2026-06-06 (topic 5) was an environment-shift: a transient vault/keychain read failure during a server restart under host load left the loadConfig secret-merge incomplete, so dashboardPin arrived unresolved and the placeholder leaked to the user. Fix extends the same {secret:true}-resilience the token path already had to the PIN, plus a never-leak-placeholder floor.
